svg.text: Introduction to Polynomials of Matrices and Applications

section.data-narration: Eigenvalues and EigenVectors are fundamental concepts in Linear Algebra that help us understand the behavior of linear transformations.
section.text: Scalars and Vectors that represent how much a linear transformation changes a vector.

section.data-narration: Polynomials of Matrices are expressions that involve powers and products of matrices. We can use polynomials to study the properties of matrices and their eigenvalues.
section.text: Expressions that involve powers and products of matrices.

section.data-narration: The Cayley-Hamilton Theorem states that every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation. This theorem is a fundamental result in Linear Algebra and has numerous applications in the field.
section.text: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation.

section.data-narration: An eigen space of a linear transformation T is the set of all vectors that are mapped to zero by T. This concept is essential in understanding the behavior of linear transformations and has numerous applications in the field.
section.text: Eigen Spaces of a Linear Transformation
The set of all vectors that are mapped to zero by T.

section.data-narration: The Cayley-Hamilton Theorem has numerous applications in the field of Linear Algebra, including solving systems of linear equations and finding the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix.
section.text: Solving systems of linear equations and finding the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix.
